title: sagina pyrenaica
intro: Sagina pyrenaica, also known as Alpine Pearlwort, is a small, delicate flowering plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Sagina pyrenaica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to treat minor cuts and scrapes.
